channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Live updates: Senate confirms Kennedy to lead HHS, moves Patel's FBI nomination forward
item.description: Embattled nominee Robert F. Kennedy Jr. received the Senate's support Thursday to lead the Department of Health and Human Services, capping a twisting journey to become the nation's next secretary.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, The Hill

item.title: Hamas says it will free 3 more hostages as planned, paving the way to resolve ceasefire dispute
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas said Thursday it would release three more Israeli hostages as planned, paving the way toward resolving a major dispute over the ceasefire in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launches fresh drone attack against Ukraine shortly after Trump-Putin phone call
item.description: Ukraine's air force indicated that there was an overnight drone attack. The news on Thursday came after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Car strikes pedestrians in Munich ahead of Vance arrival
item.description: An Afghanistan national plowed a car into a crowd of striking union workers in Munich, Germany, a day ahead of Vice President Vance’s arrival there for a security conference, according to local officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: At least 28 are hurt when a driver plows into a demonstration in Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring at least 28 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Innovators gear up work on ‘green’ hydrogen plane with plans for nonstop 9-day trip around Earth
item.description: Swiss aviation pioneer Bertrand Piccard is developing a plane powered by liquid hydrogen after a much-hyped flight in 2015 that used sunlight to travel around the world.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten And Mustakim Hasnath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia rejoices at Trump-Putin call as Zelenskyy rejects talks without Ukraine present
item.description: Russian officials and state media have taken a triumphant tone after President Donald Trump jettisoned three years of U.S. policy and announced he would likely meet soon with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate a peace deal in the almost three-year-long war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NATO is in disarray after the US announces that its security priorities lie elsewhere
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — In just one speech by U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth this week, the most powerful member of NATO has thrown the world’s biggest military alliance into disarray, raising troubling questions about America’s commitment to European security.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Car driver in Munich plows into crowd 1 day before Vance and world leaders gather for security conference
item.description: At least 20 were injured after a vehicle drove through a crowd in Munich, Germany, just hours before world leaders arrive for a security conference.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How South Korea's Constitutional Court is deciding on the president's future
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is putting up a desperate fight for his political life at Seoul’s Constitutional Court after being impeached and arrested for his short-lived imposition of martial law last year.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elephants trumpet, squeak and flap their ears after their complex move across an Australian city
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Elephants trumpeted, touched trunks and flapped their oversized ears upon reuniting with their herd after a complex, five-day move from an urban Australian zoo to a much larger enclosure.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance visits the Dachau concentration camp memorial with Holocaust survivor before Zelenskyy meeting
item.description: U_S_ Vice President JD Vance has visited the Dachau concentration camp memorial in Germany and says “seeing it up close in person really drives home what unspeakable evil was committed.”.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: With doors closed to the US, asylum-seekers turn to their Plan B: A new life in Mexico
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — When Angelica Delgado took a one-way flight to Mexico as she fled Cuba in December, she was set on seeking asylum in the United States. But after President Donald Trump effectively slammed the door on asylum-seekers crossing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: O'Reilly predicts Trump and Putin will 'get a ceasefire' in Ukraine
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy "has no power" in opposing a ceasefire deal, according to O'Reilly.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Modi is meeting with Trump in a visit meant to boost the US-India relationship and avoid tariffs
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi has met with Trump national security adviser Mike Waltz ahead of his meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: Fire authorities in Taiwan say a gas explosion at a department store killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Seoul says North Korea is destroying facility that hosted reunions of war-separated families
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea is demolishing a South Korea-built property that had been used to host reunions of families separated during the 1950-53 Korean War, the South’s government said Thursday, as it continues to eliminate symbols of engagement between the war-divided rivals.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A joke petition seeks to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congolese church leaders meet Rwanda-backed rebels in the east in latest push for peace
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s largest church organizations met Wednesday with Rwanda-backed rebels in the country’s east in the latest push for peace and dialogue after weekslong fighting that saw the rebels seize the region’s largest city of Goma.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 10 hospitalized in fire at Rio de Janeiro's Carnival costume factory
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — A fire Wednesday at a Rio de Janeiro factory that produces costumes for the city's iconic Carnival left 10 people hospitalized, Rio’s state health department said, casting a shadow over preparations for one of the world’s biggest shows.
item.dc:creator: DIARLEI RODRIGUES and ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard sworn in as top intelligence chief in Oval Office
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard was sworn in as director of national intelligence on Wednesday in the Oval Office, hours after the Senate confirmed her. Attorney General Pam Bondi administered the oath of office to Gabbard, who Trump called “an American of extraordinary courage and patriotism.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: In Ecuador's highlands, an Indigenous community faces choice between 2 candidates they don't trust
item.description: COCHAPAMBA, Ecuador (AP) — The two candidates vying for Ecuador’s presidency in a second-round run-off never campaigned in Cochapamba. Their faces are nowhere to be seen in this remote Indigenous community in the Ecuadorian highlands.
item.dc:creator: SARA ESPAÑA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Austrian far-right leader's efforts to form a new government collapse
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian far-right leader Herbert Kickl's efforts to form a coalition government with a conservative party collapsed in mutual recriminations on Wednesday, more than four months after his party won a national election.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says American among three freed in Belarus
item.description: The White House said Wednesday that President Trump secured the release of at least three people jailed in Belarus, including an American it has not named and a Belarusian journalist.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrate the Lantern Festival with high hopes and rice dumplings
item.description: NEW TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Thousands in Taiwan and China celebrated the Lantern Festival on Wednesday by releasing paper lanterns into the night sky, visiting light installations and snacking on glutinous rice dumplings.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's interim foreign minister says Russia-Iran ties to Assad are an open wound after war
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Syria's relations with Iran and Russia remain an open wound for its people after those nations backed autocratic President Bashar Assad during the long civil war, the country's new, interim foreign minister said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Egypt and Qatar are working to salvage the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: Egyptian and Qatari mediators were working to salvage the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas on Wednesday, which risks being derailed because of a dispute between the two sides, according to Egypt’s state-run Al-Qahera News TV, which is close to the country's security agencies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he and Putin agreed to begin ‘negotiations’ on ending Ukraine war
item.description: Trump says he and Russian leader Vladimir Putin have agreed to begin "negotiations" on ending the Ukraine war.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, ERIC TUCKER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Too few tents entering Gaza threatens the truce. Here’s what’s happening
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Three weeks into the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, the number of tents and temporary homes entering Gaza risks falling short of the goals set for the deal's first phase.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik in Marc Fogel exchange, official says
item.description: The United States is releasing Russian prisoner Alexander Vinnik as part of the deal to secure Marc Fogel's freedom, a Trump administration official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UN halts humanitarian work in Yemen's Houthi stronghold after staff detentions
item.description: The United Nations has suspended humanitarian operations in northern Saada after rebels detained eight more U.N. staffers, during one of the world's worst humanitarian crises.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also said that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: French girl, 11, found dead near school, murder investigation underway
item.description: Investigators in France have launched a murder investigation after a missing 11-year-old girl was found dead in a wooded area near her school.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
